title: Replace linear easing with spring physics across all DS animations
description:
ALL 28+ canvas components use transition={{ duration: 0.3 }} — flat linear/ease timing. This makes everything feel mechanical and lifeless. Apple-quality animations use spring physics.

Replace all framer-motion transitions with spring-based easing:
- Highlight state changes: spring({ damping: 25, stiffness: 400 }) — snappy
- Node insertions: spring({ damping: 20, stiffness: 300 }) — bouncy entrance
- Node removals: { duration: 0.2, ease: 'easeIn' } — quick exit
- Layout shifts (tree rebalance): spring({ damping: 30, stiffness: 200 }) — smooth repositioning

Search DataStructuresModule.tsx for all 'transition:' props and replace.

Source: framer-motion spring docs, Apple Human Interface Guidelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero instances of transition={{ duration: 0.3 }} remain
All animations use spring physics or intentional easing curves
Insert animations feel 'bouncy' — elements arrive with spring overshoot
State transitions (comparing → found) feel 'snappy' — fast settle
Animations still respect prefers-reduced-motion (DST-078)

title: Add depth and glow effects to node elements — shadows, gradients, halos
description:
Currently all nodes are flat circles/rectangles with solid borders. No depth, no glow, no gradients. This makes everything look like a wireframe, not a polished product.

Add:
- Subtle box-shadow/filter: drop-shadow on SVG nodes (2px blur, 4px spread, primary color at 20%)
- Gradient fills: instead of flat #1f2937, use radial gradient from center-light to edge-dark
- Active state glow: when a node is highlighted, add a soft glow ring (filter: blur + opacity)
- Depth layers: edges behind nodes (currently they overlap sometimes)

Reference: 3Blue1Brown manim style — nodes have subtle gradients and glow on highlight.

Source: CSS filter: drop-shadow for SVG, radialGradient SVG element.
acceptanceCriteria: All SVG nodes have subtle drop-shadow or gradient fill
Active/highlighted nodes have visible glow effect
Edges render behind nodes (proper z-ordering)
Effects are subtle — enhance, not distract
Performance acceptable (no jank on 20+ node trees)

title: Create Architex signature color palette for DS visualizations with design tokens
description:
Current DS_COLORS uses raw hex values (#6b7280, #3b82f6, etc.) that are generic Tailwind colors. No brand identity. No dark/light mode awareness. Research shows limiting to 5-7 hues maximum with 11 steps each for contrast across modes.

Design a signature 'Architex DS' palette:
- 5 semantic hues: Explore (blue), Compare (amber), Success (emerald), Danger (rose), Neutral (slate)
- Each with 11 lightness steps (50-950)
- Dark mode: steps 50-500 meet 3:1 contrast on dark surfaces
- Light mode: steps 500-950 meet 3:1 contrast on light surfaces
- Implement as CSS custom properties: --ds-explore-500, --ds-compare-300, etc.

Source: AWS Cloudscape data-vis colors, Datawrapper color guide, CleanChart 2026 palettes.
acceptanceCriteria: 5-hue semantic palette defined as CSS custom properties
11 lightness steps per hue
All DS_COLORS map values reference CSS variables
Palette works in both dark and light mode
3:1 minimum contrast ratio for all text-on-background combinations
Palette is visually distinctive — recognizable as 'Architex'

title: Add staggered entry animations when data structure first renders
description:
When navigating to a DS or clicking Random, all elements appear simultaneously. This is flat and lifeless. Apple-quality UIs use staggered animations — elements appear one by one in sequence.

Add stagger to:
- Array: cells fade in left-to-right with 30ms delay between each
- Tree: nodes appear top-down, level by level, with edges drawing after nodes
- Hash Table: buckets appear top-to-bottom with chains sliding in from left
- Bloom Filter: bits ripple in from left-to-right
- Consistent Hash Ring: ring draws first, then nodes appear, then keys animate in

Use framer-motion variants with staggerChildren.

Source: framer-motion stagger docs, Apple WWDC animation guidelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Elements appear sequentially, not all at once
Stagger delay is 20-50ms between elements (fast but perceptible)
Tree visualizations animate level-by-level (root first, then children)
Edges animate AFTER their connected nodes are visible
Random/Reset triggers the entry animation

title: Increase highlight state visibility — brighter colors, scale pulse, border glow
description:
Current highlight states add color + '20' (12.5% opacity) as background. This is barely visible, especially on dark backgrounds. From the BST screenshot, highlighted nodes look almost identical to non-highlighted ones unless zoomed in.

Increase visibility:
- Background: from color+'20' to color+'40' (25% opacity) minimum
- Border: from 2.5px solid to 3px solid with matching glow (box-shadow: 0 0 8px color)
- Scale: active node scales to 1.05x during highlight (transform: scale(1.05))
- Pulse: comparing state adds a subtle pulse animation (0.5s ease-in-out infinite alternate)

The goal: a student should be able to see which element is active from 3 feet away.
acceptanceCriteria: Highlighted elements are clearly visible from normal viewing distance
Active/comparing state has visible glow effect
Scale pulse on active elements (1.05x)
Background opacity minimum 25% (not 12.5%)
Different states (comparing vs found vs done) are visually distinct at a glance
